49.reason: This line incorrectly filters out all '0' digits, including significant zeros after the decimal point. 28.reason: [This prints the original number as-is with "e0", but scientific notation requires normalization to one non-zero digit before the decimal point. However, this branch only triggers when c[1]=='.', so it's for single-digit before decimal, but still doesn't handle trailing digits correctly in all cases. But the main issue is the outer condition.]
